title: Novo to track generic surge as semaglutide goes off patent
summary: Novo Nordisk is preparing for a surge of generic semaglutide drugs in India. The company's India head stated they will monitor rivals' pricing and market strategies closely. With many companies launching cheaper versions, Novo Nordisk plans to evaluate the...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
Mumbai: Novo Nordisk will closely track the competition as it gets ready for an explosion in generic versions of semaglutide with the blockbuster diabetes and weight-loss drug going off patent in the country, said India head Vikrant Shrotriya. With about 40 companies and more than 50 brands of cheaper generics set to enter the market, Shrotriya said the Danish innovator will remain "vigilant," taking the measure of the competition before reacting.

"When we were carving out the market position one year back, we had no clue that the 40 players will come...even you would have thought probably five-seven players will come," Shrotriya said in an interview ahead of the patent expiry on Saturday. "Now, as we are coming closer, the entire, what I call it, telescope, is converting into a microscope."

Rather than fixed targets and revenue markers, Novo plans to assess how rivals price, position and scale before responding.

"We have started seeing a situation where we will have a pitstop and we will evaluate the position how other companies have played and then see how an innovator can be placed in this kind of a situation," he said. Among the companies that have lined up generic semaglutide launches are Dr Reddy's, Sun Pharmaceuticals, Eris, Zydus, Mankind, Alkem, MSN Laboratories, Intas Pharma and Micro Labs.

Ahead of an expected price war, Shrotriya said, "We will continue to be vigilant to learn from the market and be responsible to really address it." PharmaTrac data showed that Semaglutide's market share at the end of February jumped to 21% from 13% in December, in terms of sales volume, after the company announced a price cut of as much as 37% for Wegovy in November, followed by the launch of Ozempic in December.

Novo's semaglutide brands are Ozempic and Wegovy, while the drug is called Poviztra in its marketing tie-up with Emcure. It has a collaboration with Abbott that's marketing another brand of the glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) drug.

The company struck these partnerships to expand further into India amid rival Eli Lilly grabbing the lion's share of the diabetes and weight-loss drug market with tirzepatide (Mounjaro), which combines GLP-1 with glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP), making it a dual receptor agonist. Both hormones help with production of insulin, blood sugar management and appetite control.

Experts said pricing will play the key role in deciding market share as the treatments are meant to be long term. Doctors say many patients are prone to stopping treatments within six months after initial weight loss because of cost issues and that many of them may now switch to low-cost generics.
